svg.text: From perceiving and conceiving to directly knowing
perceives the seen as the seen
Because he has not fully understood it — apariññātaṁ tassā
One who is liberated — arahaṁ / tathāgato
directly knows the seen as the seen
does not conceive [himself] in the seen
does not conceive [himself] apart from it
does not conceive the seen to be "mine"
[he] does not delight in the seen
Because he has fully understood it — pariññātaṁ tassā
The same pattern of conceiving applies across all phenomena…
conceives (maññati) → should not conceive (mā maññi) → does not conceive (na maññati)
Because he has not fully understood the phenomenon (apariññātaṁ tassā)
directly knows (abhijānāti) → should not conceive (mā maññi) → should not delight (mābhinandi)
Because he must fully understand the phenomenon (pariññeyyaṁ tassā)
directly knows (abhijānāti) → does not conceive (na maññati) → does not delight (nābhinandati)
Because he has fully understood the phenomenon
Because of the wearing away of passion, he is free from passion
Because of the wearing away of aversion, he is free from aversion
Because of the wearing away of delusion, he is free from delusion
directly knows (abhijānāti) → does not conceive (na maññati) → does not delight (nābhinandati)
Because the Tathāgata has fully understood it to the end (pariññātantaṁ tathāgatassa)
Through the complete exhaustion, fading away, cessation, giving up, and relinquishing of craving, the Tathāgata has awakened to the
unsurpassed full awakening (tathāgato sabbaso taṇhānaṁ khayā virāgā nirodhā cāgā paṭinissaggā anuttaraṁ sammāsambodhiṁ abhisambuddho)
“ Delight is the root of suffering ”
that with being [as condition], there is birth, and for one who has come into being, there is aging and death
